vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on shadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/shadow, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/shadow

triage_decision.reason: This is a standard STIG file permission hardening rule for /etc/shadow. The remediation script follows the exact pattern used in RHEL/ Rocky Linux STIGs and will not affect SSH, console access, or critical services. It only tightens permissions on a sensitive file.

previous_attempts.error_summary: LLM API error 429: {"error":{"message":"Rate limit exceeded: free-models-per-min. review_feedback: The proposed remediation plan contains an invalid chmod syntax that would fail to execute. The command 'chmod u-xwrs,g-xwrs,o-xwrt /etc/shadow' has incorrect permission specification format and would not run successfully. Additionally, there is no backup of current permissions before modification, which creates risk if the command were somehow corrected.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly follows the STIG remediation script for /etc/shadow permissions and would resolve the vulnerability. However, the remediation command syntax appears incorrect - the recommended command 'chmod u-xwrs,g-xwrs,o-xwrt /etc/shadow' has an invalid syntax (missing commas between permission sets). The correct STIG command should be 'chmod u-xw,g-rw,o-rwx /etc/shadow' or similar. The plan structure is sound but needs the correct chmod syntax.
concerns: Remediation command syntax is incorrect and would fail to execute
No backup of original file permissions before modification
suggested_improvements: Add command to backup current permissions before modification
Include verification of file accessibility after permission change
